The aircraft must be __________ before maintenance begins on the hydraulic system.
Carbon brake discs must be kept free from oil and grease __________.
All electrical power must be removed before working on the __________ system.
Engine oil consumption should be __________ at each service interval.
The hydraulic reservoir level must be __________ before each flight.
Anti-corrosion treatment must be applied to all __________ metal surfaces exposed during maintenance.
Landing gear downlock pins must be __________ before the gear is retracted.
A __________ test must be performed on the fire detection loop at each maintenance check.
Aircraft tyres are inflated with __________ to prevent moisture-related corrosion.
Borescope inspection allows technicians to view __________ components without disassembly.
The __________ manual provides step-by-step procedures for all aircraft maintenance tasks.
Non-sparking tools must be used when working __________ fuel tanks.
The torque __________ must be calibrated before use on critical fasteners.
A __________ inspection by a second independent inspector is required for critical maintenance tasks.
Engine __________ temperature exceedances must be recorded in the engine logbook.
The aircraft must be on __________ before the landing gear is retracted for maintenance.
All maintenance __________ must be retained for the period specified by the regulatory authority.
The __________ power unit provides electrical power and bleed air on the ground when engines are not running.
Composite repair patches must be designed to match the original __________ orientation of the material.
The aircraft fire extinguisher bottle pressure must be __________ before every flight.
Engine bleed air is used for aircraft __________ to maintain a comfortable cabin altitude.
Before entering a fuel tank, a __________ to enter must be obtained.
Hydraulic fluid __________ must be checked and compared against the maintenance manual limits at each service.
The aircraft's weight and __________ must be confirmed before every flight.
A __________ is used to prevent fasteners from rotating loose in a critical installation.
__________ corrosion occurs when two dissimilar metals are in electrical contact with an electrolyte present.
All maintenance personnel must complete __________ training as part of their recurrent training programme.
A __________ test on the oxygen system verifies the correct flow rate is delivered at altitude.
Aircraft technical records must be __________ and available for inspection by the regulatory authority.
The Minimum Equipment List (MEL) allows dispatch with certain __________ items.
Hydraulic actuator __________ must be replaced when the actuator is disassembled for any reason.
During aircraft towing, the nose gear must not exceed its stated __________ angle limit.
The __________ is used to verify the electrical resistance of a bonding strap.
Structural repairs must restore the original __________ and aerodynamic profile of the damaged area.
Engine __________ monitoring data is used to detect early signs of deterioration.
Before any welding on or near an aircraft, all fuel must be __________ from the area.
A __________ check drains the lowest point of the fuel tank to check for water and sediment.
The aircraft Certificate of __________ must be current for the aircraft to fly legally.
Oxygen cylinders must be stored __________ and secured against falling.
The __________ Catalogue lists all aircraft parts by assembly breakdown and part number.
The __________ prevents backflow of hydraulic fluid when a pump fails.
After a birdstrike, __________ areas including engine inlet and leading edges must be inspected.
Heat sensitive __________ plugs in aircraft wheels release to prevent tyre blowout after brake overheating.
A component that has exceeded its __________ limit must be removed regardless of apparent condition.
Fuel __________ lines must be kept clear to prevent pressurisation of tanks beyond design limits.
The __________ system removes contamination from hydraulic fluid to protect components from wear.
Before signing a task card, the certifying engineer must confirm the work was done in accordance with the __________ documentation.
A __________ leak in the pressurization system can cause rapid loss of cabin pressure.
During a __________ inspection, detailed examination of internal structure is required.
The aircraft's nose wheel steering must be __________ after a tow before releasing the aircraft to the crew.
Electrical wiring showing signs of __________ insulation must be repaired using approved materials.
Tool __________ is critical to preventing foreign object damage during maintenance.
The __________ of a repair must be assessed against allowable limits in the SRM before treatment.
Dangerous goods must be declared by the __________ and accepted by the operator before loading.
A __________ attendant must be present outside a confined space whenever personnel are working inside.
